topic: A musician hears their song being performed by someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence of my cramped apartment was suddenly shattered by a sound that felt both intimately familiar and utterly alien. It was my melody, the one I’d poured six months of sleepless nights into—the one that felt like the very marrow of my being. I froze mid-sip of lukewarm coffee, my hand hovering over the mug. Then, it came again, clearer this time, weaving through the air with a voice I didn't recognize. It was a rendition of "Starlight Echoes," my magnum opus, being performed by a stranger in a dimly lit, crowded bar across town. My heart began a frantic, uneven rhythm against my ribs. I pushed myself off the worn sofa, stumbling toward the window, needing to see something solid, anything to anchor me against this sudden, dizzying sense of dislocation.

[story 2]

I was tuning my guitar late one Tuesday night, the familiar weight of the instrument a comfort against the encroaching quiet. But the quiet dissolved when a sound drifted through the thin walls—a perfect, haunting echo of my latest composition. It was my song. I scrambled to the speakers, my breath catching in my throat as the notes flooded the room. The performance was flawless, executed with a raw, passionate intensity that belonged to someone else. I stood stock-still, my fingers twitching on the fretboard, unable to move, as if paralyzed by an invisible current. My eyes darted around the room, trying to locate the source, but every face seemed blurred, indistinct. A strange hollowness opened up in my chest, a sudden vacuum where certainty used to reside.

[story 3]

It happened during the late-night radio session. I was trying to unwind, letting the familiar cadence of my own music wash over me, when a voice cut through the static—a voice singing my lyrics, my intricate chord progressions, rendered by a completely different timbre. I sat bolt upright in my chair, the cheap vinyl groaning under my sudden shift in weight. For a long moment, I just stared at the speaker, my mouth slightly agape. It wasn't a coincidence; the phrasing, the subtle inflections—it was unmistakably mine. A slow, creeping unease settled over me, tightening my jaw. I reached for my phone, intending to check streaming services, but my fingers remained locked around the cool glass, unable to process the reality of what my ears were telling me.
